title: Adjectives with '-ji le'
explanation: Adjectives with '-ji le'. Used for expressing degree, emphasizing.

examples.pinyin: wèidào hǎo jíle !
examples.english: It tastes great!

examples.pinyin: tā de yīngwén bàng jíle 。
examples.english: His English is excellent.

examples.pinyin: hūnlǐ rènào jíle 。
examples.english: The wedding was very lively.

examples.chinese: 一点声音都没有，安静极了。
examples.pinyin: yīdiǎn shēngyīn dū méiyǒu ，ānjìng jíle 。
examples.english: There was no sound at all, and it was extremely quiet.

examples.pinyin: zuótiān de pàidùi wúliáo jíle !
examples.english: Yesterday's party was very boring!

title: Reduplication of adjectives
explanation: Reduplication of adjectives. Used for expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: nǐ de liǎn hóng hóng de 。
examples.english: Your face is red.

examples.pinyin: bǎobǎo de yǎnjīng dà dà de 。
examples.english: The baby's eyes are big.

examples.pinyin: jīnwǎn de yuèliàng yuán yuán de 。
examples.english: The moon tonight is round.

examples.pinyin: tā bàbà gāo gāo pàng pàng de 。
examples.english: Her father is tall and fat.

examples.pinyin: wǒ mèimèi shòu shòu xiǎo xiǎo de 。
examples.english: My sister is thin and small.

title: Turning adjectives into adverbs
explanation: Turning adjectives into adverbs. Used for describing actions, indicating conditions.

examples.pinyin: nǐ yào nǔlì dì xuéxí 。
examples.english: You have to study hard.

examples.pinyin: yǔtiān dì huá ，màn màn dì zǒu 。
examples.english: Sliding in the rain and walking slowly.

examples.pinyin: tā shùnlì dì tōngguò kǎoshì le 。
examples.english: He passed the exam successfully.

examples.chinese: 他认真地对我说：“我喜欢你。
examples.pinyin: tā rènzhēn dì dùi wǒ shuō ：“wǒ xǐhuān nǐ 。
examples.english: He said to me seriously: "I like you.

examples.pinyin: tā rèqíng dì yǒngbào le wǒ 。
examples.english: He embraced me warmly.

title: Expressing 'again' in the past with 'you'
explanation: Expressing 'again' in the past with 'you'. Used for referring to the past, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: yòu xiàyǔ le ！
examples.english: It's raining again!

examples.pinyin: nǐ yòu chídào le 。
examples.english: You are late again.

examples.pinyin: bǎobǎo yòu kū le 。
examples.english: The baby cried again.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yòu wàng le 。
examples.english: I forgot again.

examples.pinyin: tā yòu méi lái shàngkè 。
examples.english: He didn't come to class again.

title: Expressing 'all along' with 'yizhi'
explanation: Expressing 'all along' with 'yizhi'. Used for describing actions, referring to habitual actions, expressing frequency, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yīzhí zài xuéxí zhōngwén 。
examples.english: I have been learning Chinese.

examples.chinese: 昨天晚上我一直在做作业。
examples.pinyin: zuótiān wǎnshàng wǒ yīzhí zài zuòzuòyè 。
examples.english: I have been doing my homework last night.

examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn yīzhí hěn máng 。
examples.english: The boss has been very busy.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yīzhí hěn xǐhuān nǐ 。
examples.english: I have always liked you.

examples.pinyin: bàbà yīzhí dū bù chōuyān 。
examples.english: Dad never smokes.

title: Expressing 'always' with 'zongshi'
structure: Subj. + 总是 + Verb  /  总是 + Adv. + Adj.
explanation: Expressing 'always' with 'zongshi'. Used for expressing frequency.

examples.pinyin: tā zǒngshì chídào 。
examples.english: He is always late.

examples.pinyin: wǒ zǒngshì wàngjì zhè gè cí 。
examples.english: I always forget this word.

examples.pinyin: tā zǒngshì yīgèrén chīfàn 。
examples.english: He always eats alone.

examples.pinyin: nǐ nánpéngyǒu zǒngshì shuō zānghuà 。
examples.english: Your boyfriend always swears.

examples.chinese: 我的学生总是问我很多有意思的问题。
examples.pinyin: wǒ de xuéshēng zǒngshì wèn wǒ hěnduō yǒuyìsī de wèntí 。
examples.english: My students always ask me many interesting questions.

title: Expressing duration of inaction
structure: Subj. + (已经 +) Duration + 没 + Verb + 了
explanation: Expressing duration of inaction. Used for referring to the past, expressing duration.

examples.chinese: 他们已经两天没吃东西了。
examples.pinyin: tāmen yǐjīng liǎng tiān méi chī dōngxī le 。
examples.english: They haven't eaten for two days.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen yǐjīng shí nián méi jiàn le 。
examples.english: We haven't seen it in ten years.

examples.chinese: 他已经一个星期没洗澡了。
examples.pinyin: tā yǐjīng yī gè xīngqī méi xǐzǎo le 。
examples.english: He hasn't bathed in a week.

examples.chinese: 你多长时间没刮胡子了？
examples.pinyin: nǐ duō cháng shíjiān méi guā húzǐ le ？
examples.english: How long have you not shaved?

examples.pinyin: tā bàn gè yuè méi chūmén le 。
examples.english: She hasn't been out for half a month.

title: Expressing 'finally' with 'zhongyu'
explanation: Expressing 'finally' with 'zhongyu'. Used for expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: nǐ zhōngyú dào le ！
examples.english: You are finally here!

examples.pinyin: wǒmen zhōngyú jiànmiàn le ！
examples.english: We finally met!

examples.pinyin: nǐmen zhōngyú míngbái le 。
examples.english: You finally understand.

examples.pinyin: zuòyè zhōngyú zuò wán le ！
examples.english: The homework is finally done!

examples.pinyin: fēijī zhōngyú qǐfēi le ！
examples.english: The plane finally took off!

title: Expressing lateness with 'cai'
explanation: Expressing lateness with 'cai'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing time and date, expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 我昨天晚上十一点才到家。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zuótiān wǎnshàng shíyī diǎn cái dào jiā 。
examples.english: I didn't get home until eleven o'clock last night.

examples.pinyin: tā èrshí wǔ sùi cái shàng dàxué 。
examples.english: He didn't go to college until he was 25 years old.

examples.pinyin: tā sìshí sùi cái jiéhūn 。
examples.english: She was married at the age of forty.

examples.chinese: 我女儿昨天十二点才睡觉，作业太多了。
examples.pinyin: wǒ nv̌ér zuótiān shíèr diǎn cái shùijué ，zuòyè tài duō le 。
examples.english: My daughter went to bed at twelve o'clock yesterday and had too much homework.

examples.chinese: 你九点上班，八点半才起床？
examples.pinyin: nǐ jǐu diǎn shàngbān ，bā diǎn bàn cái qǐchuáng ？
examples.english: You go to work at nine, and you get up at half past eight?

title: Expressing 'only' with 'zhi'
explanation: Expressing 'only' with 'zhi'. Used for expressing limits.

examples.pinyin: wǒ zhǐ yǒu yī gè gēgē 。
examples.english: I have only one brother.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen zhǐ yǒu shíkuài qián 。
examples.english: We only have ten dollars.

examples.chinese: 我们公司只有两个员工。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen gōngsī zhǐ yǒu liǎnggèyuángōng 。
examples.english: Our company has only two employees.

examples.pinyin: nǐ zhǐ ài chī ròu ma ？
examples.english: Do you only love meat?

examples.pinyin: tāmen zhǐ hùi shuō yīngwén 。
examples.english: They only speak English.

title: Sequencing with 'xian' and 'zai'
explanation: Sequencing with 'xian' and 'zai'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing order, listing.

examples.pinyin: xiān xǐ shǒu zài chī 。
examples.english: Wash your hands before eating.

examples.pinyin: wǒ xǐhuān xiān xǐzǎo ，zài shùijué 。
examples.english: I like to take a bath before sleeping.

examples.pinyin: qǐng nǐ xiān mǎi piào zài jìnqù 。
examples.english: Please buy a ticket before entering.

examples.chinese: 你要先做作业，再看电视。
examples.pinyin: nǐ yào xiān zuò zuòyè ，zài kàn diànshì 。
examples.english: You have to do your homework before watching TV.

examples.pinyin: wǒ xiǎng xiān zhǎo gōngzuò ，zài bānjiā 。
examples.english: I want to find a job before moving.

title: Simultaneous tasks with 'yibian'
structure: Subj. + 一边 + Verb (，) + 一边 + Verb
explanation: Simultaneous tasks with 'yibian'. Used for referring to simultaneous events, indicating conditions.

examples.chinese: 不要一边吃东西，一边说话。
examples.pinyin: bùyào yībiān chī dōngxī，yībiān shuōhuà 。
examples.english: Don't talk while eating.

examples.chinese: 我常常一边洗澡，一边唱歌。
examples.pinyin: wǒ chángcháng yībiān xǐzǎo， yībiān chànggē 。
examples.english: I often sing while taking a bath.

examples.chinese: 孩子喜欢一边吃饭，一边玩。
examples.pinyin: háizǐ xǐhuān yībiān chīfàn， yībiān wán 。
examples.english: Children like to play while eating.

examples.chinese: 你喜欢一边听音乐，一边做作业吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ xǐhuān yībiān tīng yīnlè， yībiān zuò zuòyè ma ？
examples.english: Do you like to do your homework while listening to music?

examples.pinyin: wǒmen yībiān zǒu yībiān liáo ba 。
examples.english: Let's talk while walking.

title: Using 'always' as a complaint with 'laoshi'
explanation: Using 'always' as a complaint with 'laoshi'. Used for expressing frequency, expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: nǐ zěnyāo lǎoshì jiābān ？
examples.english: Why do you always work overtime?

examples.pinyin: nàgè háizǐ lǎo shuō zānghuà 。
examples.english: The child is always swearing.

examples.pinyin: nǐ zěnyāo lǎoshì bù gāoxīng ？
examples.english: Why are you always upset?

examples.chinese: 你儿子上课的时候老是说话。
examples.pinyin: nǐ érzǐ shàngkè de shíhòu lǎoshì shuōhuà 。
examples.english: Your son always talks during class.

examples.chinese: 这个老师老是这么严肃。
examples.pinyin: zhègè lǎoshī lǎoshì zhèyāo yánsù 。
examples.english: This teacher is always so serious.

title: Using 'cai' for small numbers
explanation: Using 'cai' for small numbers. Used for emphasizing, expressing quantity.

examples.pinyin: nǐ cái èrshí sùi ？
examples.english: Are you twenty?

examples.pinyin: zhè gè bān cái liǎng gè xuéshēng 。
examples.english: There are only two students in this class.

examples.pinyin: nǐ de gōngzī cái liǎng qiān ？
examples.english: Your salary is only two thousand?

examples.chinese: 这顿饭才二十块，太便宜了！
examples.pinyin: zhè dùn fàn cái èrshí kuài ，tài biànyí le ！
examples.english: This meal is only twenty yuan, so cheap!

examples.pinyin: nǐ cái yībǎi jīn ，huán yào jiǎnféi ？
examples.english: You're only a hundred pounds and you still have to lose weight?

title: Adjectives with 'name' and 'zheme'
structure: Subj. + 那么 / 这么 + Adj.
explanation: Adjectives with 'name' and 'zheme'. Used for describing things, adding more explanation, expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 他说得这么快，你们听得懂吗？
examples.pinyin: tā shuōdé zhèyāo kuài ，nǐmen tīng dé dǒng ma ？
examples.english: He speaks so fast, do you understand?

examples.chinese: 我没想到这个考试这么难。
examples.pinyin: wǒ méi xiǎngdào zhègè kǎoshì zhèyāo nán 。
examples.english: I didn't expect this exam to be so difficult.

examples.pinyin: yǔ zhèyāo dà，míngtiān zài qù ba 。
examples.english: The rain is so heavy, go again tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 你那么聪明，一定能猜到。
examples.pinyin: nǐ nàyāo cōngmíng ，yīdìng néng cāidào 。
examples.english: You are so smart, you can guess.

examples.chinese: 老板那么忙，肯定没时间。
examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn nàyāo máng ，kěndìng méi shíjiān 。
examples.english: The boss is so busy, there must be no time.

title: Basic comparisons with 'yiyang'
structure: Noun 1 + 跟 / 和 + Noun 2 + 一样 （+ Adj.）
explanation: Basic comparisons with 'yiyang'. Used for comparing, expressing similarity.

examples.pinyin: wǒ hé nǐ yīyáng 。
examples.english: Me too.

examples.chinese: 他的性格跟他妈妈一样。
examples.pinyin: tā de xìnggé gēn tā māmā yīyáng 。
examples.english: His character is the same as his mother.

examples.chinese: 北京的天气和上海不一样。
examples.pinyin: běijīng de tiānqì hé shànghǎi bù yīyáng 。
examples.english: The weather in Beijing is different from Shanghai.

examples.chinese: 这个词的意思和那个词一样吗？
examples.pinyin: zhè gè cí de yìsī hé nà gè cí yīyáng ma ？
examples.english: Does this word mean the same as that word?

examples.chinese: 美国文化跟中国文化不一样。
examples.pinyin: měiguó wénhuà gēn zhōngguó wénhuà bù yīyáng 。
examples.english: American culture is different from Chinese culture.

title: Expressing 'both A and B' with 'you'
structure: Subj. + 又 + Adj. 1 + 又 + Adj. 2
explanation: Expressing 'both A and B' with 'you'. Used for describing things, expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: tā nánpéngyǒu yòu gāo yòu shuài 。
examples.english: Her boyfriend is tall and handsome.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè fángzǐ yòu dà yòu liàng 。
examples.english: This house is big and bright.

examples.pinyin: māmā de tóufā yòu hēi yòu liàng 。
examples.english: Mom's hair is dark and shiny.

examples.pinyin: wǒ jiějiě yòu cōngmíng yòu piāoliàng 。
examples.english: My sister is smart and beautiful.

examples.pinyin: zhōngguó cài yòu biànyí yòu hǎochī 。
examples.english: Chinese food is cheap and delicious.

title: Expressing 'rather' with 'bijiao'
explanation: Expressing 'rather' with 'bijiao'. Used for comparing.

examples.pinyin: zhègè wèntí bǐjiào jiǎndān 。
examples.english: This question is relatively simple.

examples.chinese: 我觉得这个价格比较贵。
examples.pinyin: wǒ juédé zhègè jiàgé bǐjiào gùi 。
examples.english: I think this price is more expensive.

examples.pinyin: wǒ jiā lí shìzhōngxīn bǐjiào jìn 。
examples.english: My home is near the city center.

examples.chinese: 大城市的工作压力比较大。
examples.pinyin: dà chéngshì de gōngzuò yālì bǐjiào dà 。
examples.english: Work pressure in large cities is relatively high.

examples.chinese: 你上班坐地铁比较方便吧？
examples.pinyin: nǐ shàngbān zuò dìtiě bǐjiào fāngbiàn ba ？
examples.english: Is it more convenient for you to take the subway to work?

title: Intensifying with 'duo'
explanation: Intensifying with 'duo'. Used for adding more explanation, emphasizing.

examples.pinyin: yīgèrén duō hǎo！
examples.english: How nice a person is!

examples.pinyin: nǐ nv̌ér duō cōngmíng a！
examples.english: How smart is your daughter!

examples.pinyin: jīntiān tiānqì duō shūfú ！
examples.english: How comfortable the weather is today!

examples.pinyin: nǐ kàn zhè gè dìfāng ，duō měi a！
examples.english: Look at this place, how beautiful!

examples.pinyin: xué zhōngwén duō yǒuyìsī a！
examples.english: How interesting is learning Chinese!

title: Modifying nouns with adjective + 'de'
explanation: Modifying nouns with adjective + 'de'. Used for describing people, describing things, describing places, expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: piāoliàng de nv̌háiér
examples.english: Pretty girl

examples.pinyin: là de cài
examples.english: Spicy dishes

examples.pinyin: kěài de bǎobǎo
examples.english: cute baby

examples.pinyin: wǒ xǐhuān xīnxiān de guǒzhī 。
examples.english: I like fresh juice.

examples.pinyin: tā chángcháng mǎi biànyí de dōngxī 。
examples.english: He often buys cheap things.

title: Expressing 'or' in statements
explanation: Expressing 'or' in statements. Used for giving alternatives.

examples.pinyin: wǒ hē kāfēi huòzhě chá ，dū xíng 。
examples.english: I can drink coffee or tea.

examples.chinese: 星期六或者星期天，都可以。
examples.pinyin: xīngqīlìu huòzhě xīngqītiān ， dū kěyǐ 。
examples.english: Saturday or Sunday.

examples.chinese: 今天晚上我想吃披萨或者寿司。
examples.pinyin: jīntiān wǎnshàng wǒ xiǎng chī pīsà huòzhě shòusī 。
examples.english: I want to eat pizza or sushi tonight.

examples.chinese: 周末的时候，我喜欢在家做饭或者看电影。
examples.pinyin: zhōumò de shíhòu，wǒ xǐhuān zài jiā zuòfàn huòzhě kàn diànyǐng 。
examples.english: On the weekend, I like to cook or watch movies at home.

examples.pinyin: nǐ qù huòzhě tā qù ，dū kěyǐ 。
examples.english: You can go or she can.

title: Expressing 'then…' with 'name'
explanation: Expressing 'then…' with 'name'. Used for connecting ideas.

examples.chinese: 他不听，那我应该怎么办？
examples.pinyin: tā bù tīng ，nà wǒ yìnggāi zěnyāobàn ？
examples.english: He does n’t listen, what should I do?

examples.chinese: 你说这个办法不行，那你有别的办法吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ shuō zhègè bànfǎ bùxíng ，nà nǐ yǒu biéde bànfǎ ma ？
examples.english: You said that this method will not work, then do you have another way?

examples.chinese: 大家都到了，那么我们开始吧。
examples.pinyin: dàjiā dū dào le ，nàyāo wǒmen kāishǐ ba 。
examples.english: Everyone is here, so let's get started.

examples.pinyin: yǔ tíng le ，nà wǒmen zǒu ba 。
examples.english: The rain has stopped, then let's go.

examples.chinese: 你说得有道理，那么就这么办吧。
examples.pinyin: nǐ shuō dé yǒu dàolǐ ，nàyāo jìu zhèyāo bàn ba 。
examples.english: You make sense, so do it.

title: Expressing 'with' with 'gen'
structure: Subj. + 跟 + Person + 一起 + Verb + Obj.  /  Subj. + 跟 + Person + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'with' with 'gen'

examples.chinese: 我昨天跟朋友去海滩了。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zuótiān gēn péngyǒu qù hǎitān le 。
examples.english: I went to the beach with my friend yesterday.

examples.pinyin: bùyào gēn wǒ shuōhuà！
examples.english: Do not talk to me!

examples.chinese: 我明天要跟新客户见面。
examples.pinyin: wǒ míngtiān yào gēn xīn kèhù jiànmiàn 。
examples.english: I have to meet new customers tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 你什么时候跟你女朋友结婚？
examples.pinyin: nǐ shíyāo shíhòu gēn nǐ nv̌péngyǒu jiéhūn ？
examples.english: When will you marry your girlfriend?

examples.chinese: 你喜欢跟你父母聊天吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ xǐhuān gēn nǐ fùmǔ liáotiān ma ？
examples.english: Do you like chatting with your parents?

title: Offering choices with 'haishi'
structure: ( Subj. + Verb + ) Option A + 还是 + Option B ?
explanation: Offering choices with 'haishi'. Used for asking questions, giving alternatives.

examples.pinyin: wǒ huánshì tā ？
examples.english: Am I still him?

examples.pinyin: yī gè huánshì liǎng gè ？
examples.english: One or two?

examples.pinyin: là de huánshì bù là de ？
examples.english: Spicy or not?

examples.pinyin: bīng de huánshì rè de ？
examples.english: Iced or hot?

examples.pinyin: shànghǎi huánshì běijīng ？
examples.english: Shanghai or Beijing?

title: Before a specific time with 'yiqian'
explanation: Before a specific time with 'yiqian'. Used for referring to the past, sequencing events in time, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: chīfàn yǐqián ，nǐ xǐ shǒu le ma ？
examples.english: Did you wash your hands before eating?

examples.pinyin: shùijué yǐqián ，bùyào chī dōngxī 。
examples.english: Don't eat anything before going to bed.

examples.pinyin: liǎng nián yǐqián ， nǐ rènshì tā ma ？
examples.english: Did you know him two years ago?

examples.chinese: 星期五以前，你要做完这些工作。
examples.pinyin: xīngqīwǔ yǐqián ，nǐ yào zuò wán zhèxiē gōngzuò 。
examples.english: Before Friday, you have to finish these tasks.

examples.chinese: 上大学以前，你来过上海吗？
examples.pinyin: shàng dàxué yǐqián，nǐ lái guò shànghǎi ma ？
examples.english: Have you been to Shanghai before going to university?

title: Expressing 'before' in general with 'yiqian'
structure: 以前 + Subj. + Verb + Obj.  /   Subj. + 以前 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'before' in general with 'yiqian'. Used for referring to the past, sequencing events in time, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: yǐqián wǒ bù hùi shuō zhōngwén 。
examples.english: I couldn't speak Chinese before.

examples.pinyin: nǐ yǐqián zhù zài nǎér ？
examples.english: Where did you live before?

examples.pinyin: yǐqián wǒ bù rènshì tā 。
examples.english: I didn't know him before.

examples.chinese: 以前他是我们的客户吗？
examples.pinyin: yǐqián tā shì wǒmen de kèhù ma ？
examples.english: Was he our customer before?

examples.chinese: 以前他是一个服务员，现在是老板。
examples.pinyin: yǐqián tā shì yī gè fúwùyuán，xiànzài shì lǎobǎn 。
examples.english: He used to be a waiter and now he is the boss.

title: Expressing 'just now' with 'gangcai'
explanation: Expressing 'just now' with 'gangcai'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing time and date, referring to changes of situation.

examples.pinyin: nǐ gāngcái shuō shíyāo le ？
examples.english: What did you just say?

examples.pinyin: gāngcái shúi lái le ？
examples.english: Who was here just now?

examples.pinyin: gāngcái nǐ qù nǎér le ？
examples.english: Where did you go just now?

examples.pinyin: gāngcái wǒ qù xǐshǒujiān le 。
examples.english: I went to the bathroom just now.

examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn gāngcái shēngqì le 。
examples.english: The boss was angry just now.

title: Expressing 'when' with 'shi'
explanation: Expressing 'when' with 'shi'. Used for referring to simultaneous events, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: kǎoshì shí bùyào shuōhuà 。
examples.english: Don't talk during the exam.

examples.chinese: 我面试时经常会问这个问题。
examples.pinyin: wǒ miànshì shí jīngcháng hùi wèn zhègè wèntí 。
examples.english: I often ask this question during interviews.

examples.pinyin: hùiyì jiéshù shí lǎobǎn cái lái 。
examples.english: The boss came at the end of the meeting.

examples.pinyin: wǒ shēngqì shí bù xiǎng shuōhuà 。
examples.english: I don't want to talk when I'm angry.

examples.chinese: 他们大学毕业时就分手了。
examples.pinyin: tāmen dàxué bìyè shí jìu fēnshǒu le 。
examples.english: They broke up when they graduated from college.

title: In the future in general with 'yihou'
structure: Subj. + 以后 + Verb + Obj.  /  Subj. + 以后 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: In the future in general with 'yihou'. Used for referring to the future, sequencing events in time.

examples.pinyin: yǐhòu nǐmen hùi xiǎngwǒ ma ？
examples.english: Will you miss me in the future?

examples.pinyin: yǐhòu nǐ xiǎng lái zhōngguó ma ？
examples.english: Do you want to come to China in the future?

examples.pinyin: wǒ yǐhòu bù hējǐu le 。
examples.english: I won't drink anymore.

examples.chinese: 我们以后不在这里工作了。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen yǐhòu bù zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò le 。
examples.english: We will not work here anymore.

examples.pinyin: nǐérzǐ yǐhòu xiǎng zuò shíyāo ？
examples.english: What does your son want to do in the future?

title: Sequencing past events with 'houlai'
explanation: Sequencing past events with 'houlai'. Used for referring to the past, sequencing events in time, expressing order.

examples.chinese: 他说他会来，后来又说不来了。
examples.pinyin: tā shuō tā hùi lái ，hòulái yòu shuō bù lái le 。
examples.english: He said he would come, but then he couldn't.

examples.chinese: 他们上周吵架了，后来和好了。
examples.pinyin: tāmen shàngzhōu chǎojià le ，hòulái héhǎo le 。
examples.english: They quarreled last week, and later they reconciled.

examples.chinese: 他以前是厨师，后来当了老板。
examples.pinyin: tā yǐqián shì chúshī ，hòulái dāng le lǎobǎn 。
examples.english: He used to be a cook and later became a boss.

examples.chinese: 我昨晚走得早，不知道后来发生了什么。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zuówǎn zǒu dé zǎo ，bù zhīdào hòulái fāshēng le shíyāo 。
examples.english: I walked early last night and I don't know what happened later.

examples.chinese: 开始我父母不同意，后来他们同意了。
examples.pinyin: kāishǐ wǒ fùmǔ bù tóngyì ，hòulái tāmen tóngyì le 。
examples.english: At first my parents disagreed, and later they agreed.

title: Approximating with sequential numbers
explanation: Approximating with sequential numbers. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.pinyin: yī liǎng tiān
examples.english: A day or two

examples.pinyin: sān sì gè rén
examples.english: Three or four people

examples.chinese: 老板昨天喝了七八瓶胡萝卜汁。
examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn zuótiān hē leqī bā píng húluóbǔ zhī 。
examples.english: The boss drank seven or eight bottles of carrot juice yesterday.

examples.chinese: 这个词我们学过两三次了。
examples.pinyin: zhè gè cí wǒmen xué guò liǎng sān cì le 。
examples.english: We have learned this word two or three times.

examples.pinyin: wǒ děng le nǐ sān sì gè xiǎoshí 。
examples.english: I waited for you three or four hours.

title: Aspect particle 'zhe'
explanation: Aspect particle 'zhe'. Used for indicating conditions, expressing duration.

examples.pinyin: wǒ dú ，nǐ tīng zháo 。
examples.english: I read, you listen.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen zuò ，nǐmen kàn zháo 。
examples.english: We do, you guys watch.

examples.pinyin: nǐmen zuò zháo ，wǒ mǎshàng húilái 。
examples.english: You sit down, and I will come back immediately.

examples.chinese: 我出去一下，你帮我看着行李。
examples.pinyin: wǒ chūqù yīxià ，nǐ bāng wǒ kàn zháo xínglǐ 。
examples.english: Let me go out, you help me look at the luggage.

examples.pinyin: tā xǐhuān zhàn zháo chīfàn 。
examples.english: She likes to stand and eat.

title: Change of state with 'le'
structure: Subj. (+ 不) + Verb / Adj. + 了
explanation: Change of state with 'le'. Used for indicating conditions, expressing result, referring to changes of situation.

examples.pinyin: wǒ è le 。
examples.english: I'm hungry.

examples.pinyin: háizǐmen dū lèi le 。
examples.english: The children are tired.

examples.pinyin: māmā lǎo le 。
examples.english: Mom is old.

examples.pinyin: nǐ pàng le 。
examples.english: You are fat.

examples.pinyin: nǐ shìbùshì shēngqì le ？
examples.english: Are you angry?

title: Questions with 'ne'
explanation: Questions with 'ne'. Used for asking questions, expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: nǐ hǎo ma ？wǒ hěn hǎo 。
examples.english: How are you? I'm very good.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yě hěn hǎo 。
examples.english: I am fine too.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè hěn hǎo ， nà gè ní ？
examples.english: This is good, what about that?

examples.pinyin: zhè gè yòng zhōngwén zěnyāo shuō ？
examples.english: How do you say this in Chinese?

examples.pinyin: wǒ zài jiā，nǐ ní ？
examples.english: I'm at home, how about you?

title: Structural particle 'de'
structure: 的 + Noun  /  Verb + 得  /  Adj. + 地 + Verb
explanation: Structural particle 'de'

examples.pinyin: xiǎolǐ de fángzǐ
examples.english: Xiao Li's house

examples.pinyin: hóngsè de zìxíngchē
examples.english: Red bicycle

examples.pinyin: wǒ de shǒujī
examples.english: My cell phone

examples.pinyin: wǒmen de lǎoshī
examples.english: our teacher

examples.pinyin: piāoliàng de yīfú
examples.english: beautiful clothes

title: Expressing 'about' with 'guanyu'
explanation: Expressing 'about' with 'guanyu'. Used for expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 关于中国历史，我知道的不多。
examples.pinyin: guānyú zhōngguó lìshǐ，wǒ zhīdào de bù duō 。
examples.english: I do n’t know much about Chinese history.

examples.chinese: 关于这次考试，我还有几个问题要问。
examples.pinyin: guānyú zhè cì kǎoshì，wǒ huán yǒu jī gè wèntí yào wèn 。
examples.english: I have a few questions to ask about this exam.

examples.chinese: 关于这个问题，你们还是问他吧。
examples.pinyin: guānyú zhègè wèntí，nǐmen huánshì wèn tā ba 。
examples.english: Regarding this question, you should ask him.

examples.chinese: 关于你的建议，老板还在考虑。
examples.pinyin: guānyú nǐ de jiànyì，lǎobǎn huán zài kǎolv̀ 。
examples.english: Regarding your suggestion, the boss is still considering.

examples.chinese: 关于调查结果，我们也不太清楚。
examples.pinyin: guānyú diàochá jiéguǒ，wǒmen yě bù tài qīngchǔ 。
examples.english: We are not very clear about the results of the investigation.

title: Expressing 'all the way until' with 'zhidao'
structure: 直到 + Time / Event ，Subj. + 都 ( + 不 / 没 ) ⋯⋯
explanation: Expressing 'all the way until' with 'zhidao'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 直到十二点半，我才做完作业。
examples.pinyin: zhídào shíèr diǎn bàn ，wǒ cái zuò wán zuòyè 。
examples.english: I didn't finish my homework until half past twelve.

examples.chinese: 直到半个月以后，我的感冒才好。
examples.pinyin: zhídào bàn gè yuè yǐhòu ，wǒ de gǎnmào cái hǎo 。
examples.english: It was not until half a month later that my cold was good.

examples.chinese: 她直到十八岁才见到亲生父母。
examples.pinyin: tā zhídào shíbā sùi cái jiàndào qīnshēng fùmǔ 。
examples.english: She did not meet her biological parents until she was eighteen.

examples.chinese: 我直到大学毕业才开始独立生活。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zhídào dàxué bìyè cái kāishǐ dúlì shēnghuó 。
examples.english: I didn't start to live independently until I graduated from college.

examples.chinese: 直到去了北京，他才知道一个人在大城市多辛苦。
examples.pinyin: zhídào qù le běijīng ，tā cái zhīdào yīgèrén zài dà chéngshì duō xīnkǔ 。
examples.english: He didn't know how hard a person was in a big city until he went to Beijing.

title: Expressing 'towards' with 'xiang'
explanation: Expressing 'towards' with 'xiang'

examples.pinyin: xiàng dōng zǒu 。
examples.english: Go east.

examples.pinyin: xiàng qián kàn 。
examples.english: Look forward.

examples.pinyin: xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn 。
examples.english: turn left.

examples.pinyin: lǎoshī zhèng xiàng wǒmen zǒu lái 。
examples.english: The teacher is coming to us.

examples.chinese: 火车已经开了，她还在向我招手。
examples.pinyin: huǒchē yǐjīng kāi le ，tā huán zài xiàng wǒ zhāoshǒu 。
examples.english: The train has already started and she is still waving to me.

title: Appearance with 'kanqilai'
explanation: Appearance with 'kanqilai'. Used for describing things, expressing attitude.

examples.pinyin: dàngāo kànqǐlái hěn hǎochī 。
examples.english: The cake looks delicious.

examples.pinyin: zhè jiā cāntīng kànqǐlái bùcuò 。
examples.english: This restaurant looks good.

examples.chinese: 你男朋友看起来很成熟。
examples.pinyin: nǐ nánpéngyǒu kànqǐlái hěn chéngshú 。
examples.english: Your boyfriend looks mature.

examples.chinese: 老板看起来有点不高兴。
examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn kànqǐlái yǒudiǎn bù gāoxīng 。
examples.english: The boss looks a little unhappy.

examples.pinyin: nǐmen kànqǐlái chàbùduō gāo 。
examples.english: You guys look almost tall.

title: Basic comparisons with 'meiyou'
explanation: Basic comparisons with 'meiyou'. Used for denying and refusing, comparing.

examples.pinyin: xiǎozhāng méiyǒu xiǎolǐ gāo 。
examples.english: Xiao Zhang does not have Xiao Li Gao.

examples.pinyin: xiǎolǐ méiyǒu xiǎozhāng ǎi 。
examples.english: Xiao Li does not have Xiao Zhang short.

examples.chinese: 这个地方没有上海好玩。
examples.pinyin: zhè gè dìfāng méiyǒu shànghǎi hǎowán 。
examples.english: This place is not as fun as Shanghai.

examples.pinyin: huǒchē méiyǒu fēijī kuài 。
examples.english: The train is not as fast as the plane.

examples.pinyin: tā méiyǒu tādìdì cōngmíng 。
examples.english: He is not as smart as his brother.

title: Causative verbs
explanation: Causative verbs. Used for sentence patterns, giving commands.

examples.pinyin: nǐ wèishíyāo bù ràng wǒ qù ？
examples.english: Why don't you let me go?

examples.pinyin: zhè bù diànyǐng ràng rén hěn gǎndòng 。
examples.english: This movie is very touching.

examples.chinese: 那个老人让我想到了我爷爷。
examples.pinyin: nàgè lǎorén ràng wǒ xiǎngdào le wǒ yéyé 。
examples.english: The old man reminded me of my grandpa.

examples.chinese: 他不听话，你叫我怎么办？
examples.pinyin: tā bù tīnghuà ，nǐ jiào wǒ zěnyāobàn ？
examples.english: He is disobedient, what should you do if you tell me?

examples.chinese: 他偷偷约别的女孩，你叫我怎么想？
examples.pinyin: tā tōutōu yuē biéde nv̌hái ，nǐ jiào wǒ zěnyāo xiǎng ？
examples.english: He secretly asked other girls, what do you think of me?

title: Expressing 'through' with 'jingguo'
explanation: Expressing 'through' with 'jingguo'. Used for referring to past experiences.

examples.chinese: 我每天上班都经过这里。
examples.pinyin: wǒ měitiān shàngbān dū jīngguò zhèlǐ 。
examples.english: I pass here every day at work.

examples.chinese: 我坐火车回老家常常经过这个城市。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zuò huǒchē húi lǎojiā chángcháng jīngguò zhègè chéngshì 。
examples.english: I often pass through the city by train back home.

examples.chinese: 你经过我家门口的时候叫我一下。
examples.pinyin: nǐ jīngguò wǒ jiā ménkǒu de shíhòu jiào wǒ yīxià 。
examples.english: Call me when you pass by my door.

examples.chinese: 她经过这个咖啡店的时候就会想到他。
examples.pinyin: tā jīngguò zhègè kāfēidiàn de shíhòu jìu hùi xiǎngdào tā 。
examples.english: She thinks of him when she passes this coffee shop.

examples.chinese: 经过自己的努力，她考上了北京大学。
examples.pinyin: jīngguò zìjǐ de nǔlì ，tā kǎoshàng le běijīng dàxué 。
examples.english: After her own efforts, she was admitted to Peking University.

title: Expressing 'should' with 'yinggai'
structure: Subj. (+ 不) + 应该 + Verb + Obj.
explanation: Expressing 'should' with 'yinggai'. Used for expressing obligations.

examples.pinyin: zài zhōngguó， nǐ yìnggāi hē báijǐu 。
examples.english: In China, you should drink liquor.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yìnggāi gěi nǐ duōshǎo qián ？
examples.english: How much should I give you?

examples.chinese: 感冒的时候应该喝热水。
examples.pinyin: gǎnmào de shíhòu yìnggāi hē rèshǔi 。
examples.english: Drink hot water when you have a cold.

examples.chinese: 明天你应该八点半来公司。
examples.pinyin: míngtiān nǐ yìnggāi bā diǎn bàn lái gōngsī 。
examples.english: You should come to the company at half past eight tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 他太累了，应该回家休息。
examples.pinyin: tā tài lèi le，yìnggāi húijiā xīuxī 。
examples.english: He is too tired and should go home to rest.

title: Expressing 'difficult' with 'nan'
explanation: Expressing 'difficult' with 'nan'. Used for describing actions.

examples.pinyin: zhè jù huà hěn nán dǒng 。
examples.english: This sentence is hard to understand.

examples.pinyin: hànyǔ hěn nán xué 。
examples.english: Chinese is difficult to learn.

examples.pinyin: zhōngguócài hěn nán zuò 。
examples.english: Chinese food is hard to make.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè dōngxī xiànzài hěn nán mǎi 。
examples.english: This thing is hard to buy now.

examples.pinyin: zhè gè hànzì hěn nán xiě 。
examples.english: This Chinese character is difficult to write.

title: Expressing duration with 'le'
explanation: Expressing duration with 'le'. Used for referring to the past, expressing duration, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: tā xué le yī nián zhōngwén 。
examples.english: He studied Chinese for a year.

examples.pinyin: wǒ kàn le yī gè wǎnshàng shū 。
examples.english: I read the book for one night.

examples.pinyin: tā zuò le bā nián jīnglǐ 。
examples.english: He has been a manager for eight years.

examples.chinese: 我们坐了十五个小时的飞机。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen zuò le shíwǔ gè xiǎoshí de fēijī 。
examples.english: We flew for fifteen hours.

examples.pinyin: nǎinǎi kàn le yī tiān de diànshì 。
examples.english: Grandma watched TV all day.

title: Expressing ongoing duration with double 'le'
structure: Subj. + Verb + 了 + Duration + 了
explanation: Expressing ongoing duration with double 'le'. Used for referring to the past, expressing duration, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: nǐ shùi le yī tiān le 。
examples.english: You have slept all day.

examples.pinyin: tā zài běijīng zhù le liǎng nián le 。
examples.english: He has lived in Beijing for two years.

examples.chinese: 这个会，他们开了两个小时了。
examples.pinyin: zhè gè hùi，tāmen kāi le liǎng gè xiǎoshí le 。
examples.english: At this meeting, they had two hours.

examples.chinese: 我在这儿等了半个小时了。
examples.pinyin: wǒ zài zhèér děng le bàn gè xiǎoshí le 。
examples.english: I have been waiting here for half an hour.

examples.chinese: 他们在酒吧待了一个晚上了。
examples.pinyin: tāmen zài jǐuba dài le yī gè wǎnshàng le 。
examples.english: They spent the night in the bar.

title: Inability with 'mei banfa'
explanation: Inability with 'mei banfa'. Used for describing actions, indicating conditions, expressing impossibility.

examples.chinese: 今天太忙了，中午没办法出去吃饭。
examples.pinyin: jīntiān tài máng le，zhōngwǔ méi bànfǎ chūqù chīfàn 。
examples.english: Today is too busy to eat out at noon.

examples.chinese: 他没带手机，没办法给我打电话。
examples.pinyin: tā méi dài shǒujī，méi bànfǎ gěi wǒ dǎdiànhuà 。
examples.english: He didn't bring his cell phone, he couldn't call me.

examples.chinese: 没有水，我们没办法洗衣服。
examples.pinyin: méiyǒu shǔi，wǒmen méi bànfǎ xǐ yīfú 。
examples.english: Without water, we can't wash clothes.

examples.chinese: 这里太吵了，我没办法工作。
examples.pinyin: zhèlǐ tài chǎo le，wǒ méi bànfǎ gōngzuò 。
examples.english: It's too noisy here, I can't work.

examples.chinese: 他不开门，所以她没办法进去。
examples.pinyin: tā bù kāimén，suǒyǐ tā méi bànfǎ jìnqù 。
examples.english: He didn't open the door, so she couldn't get in.

title: Reduplication of verbs
explanation: Reduplication of verbs. Used for expressing duration, decreasing formality.

examples.pinyin: nǐ kànkàn 。
examples.english: Take a look.

examples.pinyin: wǒ shì shì 。
examples.english: Let me try.

examples.pinyin: shuō shuō nǐ de xiǎngfǎ 。
examples.english: Tell me your thoughts.

examples.pinyin: chūqù wán wán ba！
examples.english: Go out and play!

examples.pinyin: wǒ xiǎng chūqù zǒu zǒu 。
examples.english: I want to go for a walk.

title: Special verbs with 'hen'
explanation: Special verbs with 'hen'. Used for expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: wǒ hěn xiǎng nǐ 。
examples.english: I miss you.

examples.pinyin: tā hěn xǐhuān nǐ 。
examples.english: He likes you very much.

examples.pinyin: nǐyìnggāihěn xiǎoxīn 。
examples.english: You should be very careful.

examples.chinese: 我很怕晚上一个人在家。
examples.pinyin: wǒ hěn pà wǎnshàng yīgèrén zàijiā 。
examples.english: I'm scared to be alone at night.

examples.chinese: 你应该很了解自己的孩子。
examples.pinyin: nǐ yìnggāi hěn lejiě zìjǐ de háizǐ 。
examples.english: You should know your child well.

title: Direction complement
explanation: Direction complement. Used for describing actions, expressing movement, describing places.

examples.pinyin: wǒ zài lóushàng ，nǐ shànglái 。
examples.english: I'm upstairs, you come up.

examples.pinyin: nǐ zài lóushàng děng wǒ yīxià 。
examples.english: You are waiting for me upstairs.

examples.chinese: 出来玩吧，我们在酒吧等你。
examples.pinyin: chūlái wán ba ，wǒmen zài jǐuba děng nǐ 。
examples.english: Come out to play, we are waiting for you at the bar.

examples.pinyin: wǒ mā bù ràng wǒ chūqù 。
examples.english: My mom won't let me go out.

examples.chinese: 这是我家，进来吧，随便坐。
examples.pinyin: zhè shì wǒjiā ，jìnlái ba ，súibiàn zuò 。
examples.english: This is my home. Come in and sit casually.

title: Potential complement
structure: Subj. + Verb + 得 / 不 + Complement
explanation: Potential complement. Used for describing actions.

examples.chinese: 你没戴眼镜，看得清楚吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ méi dài yǎnjìng ，kàn dé qīngchǔ ma ？
examples.english: You do n’t wear glasses, can you see clearly?

examples.pinyin: tā zhèyāo cūxīn ，zuò dé hǎo ma ？
examples.english: He is so careless, is he doing well?

examples.chinese: 你这么聪明，肯定学得会。
examples.pinyin: nǐ zhèyāo cōngmíng ，kěndìng xué dé hùi 。
examples.english: If you are so smart, you will definitely learn it.

examples.chinese: 早上五点出发，孩子们起得来吗？
examples.pinyin: zǎoshàng wǔdiǎn chūfā ，háizǐmen qǐ dé lái ma ？
examples.english: Will the children get up at five in the morning?

examples.chinese: 这么高的山，你爬得上去吗？
examples.pinyin: zhèyāo gāo de shān ，nǐ pá dé shàngqù ma ？
examples.english: Can you climb such a high mountain?

title: Result complements
explanation: Result complements. Used for describing actions, expressing result.

examples.pinyin: nǐ chī hǎo le ma ？
examples.english: Have you eaten

examples.pinyin: dùibùqǐ ，wǒ jì cuò le shíjiān 。
examples.english: Sorry, I remembered the wrong time.

examples.chinese: 你来晚了，我们已经关门了。
examples.pinyin: nǐ lái wǎn le ，wǒmen yǐjīng guānmén le 。
examples.english: You are late, we are already closed.

examples.pinyin: tā wán huài le gēgē de wánjù 。
examples.english: He broke his brother's toy.

examples.pinyin: wǒ méi chī bǎo 。
examples.english: I am not full.

title: Result complement '-xiaqu'
structure: Subj. + Verb （+ 不） + 下去 (+ 了)
explanation: Result complement '-xiaqu'. Used for describing actions, expressing result.

examples.pinyin: shuō xiàqù 。
examples.english: Keep talking.

examples.chinese: 这本书你一定要认真地看下去。
examples.pinyin: zhè běn shū nǐ yīdìng yào rènzhēn dì kàn xiàqù 。
examples.english: You must read this book carefully.

examples.chinese: 这样做很有意义，你应该做下去。
examples.pinyin: zhèyáng zuò hěn yǒu yìyì ，nǐ yìnggāi zuò xiàqù 。
examples.english: It makes sense to do so, you should do it.

examples.chinese: 他走了，因为他没脸待下去了。
examples.pinyin: tā zǒu le ，yīnwèi tā méiliǎn dài xiàqù le 。
examples.english: He was gone because he was faceless.

examples.chinese: 你不能这样生活下去了。
examples.pinyin: nǐ bùnéng zhèyáng shēnghuó xiàqù le 。
examples.english: You can't live like this anymore.

title: Expressing 'as long as' with 'zhiyao'
explanation: Expressing 'as long as' with 'zhiyao'. Used for indicating conditions, indicating cause and effect.

examples.chinese: 只要你真的喜欢，我就给你买。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyào nǐ zhēnde xǐhuān， wǒ jìu gěi nǐ mǎi 。
examples.english: As long as you really like it, I will buy it for you.

examples.chinese: 只要你努力学习，就会有进步。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyào nǐ nǔlì xuéxí， jìu hùi yǒu jìnbù 。
examples.english: As long as you study hard, there will be progress.

examples.chinese: 只要有你在，我就放心了。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyào yǒu nǐ zài， wǒ jìu fàngxīn le 。
examples.english: As long as you are there, I can rest assured.

examples.chinese: 只要下雨，她就会心情不好。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyào xiàyǔ， tā jìu hùi xīnqíng bù hǎo 。
examples.english: As long as it rains, she will be in a bad mood.

examples.chinese: 只要看到妈妈，宝宝就不哭了。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyào kàndào māmā， bǎobǎo jìu bù kū le 。
examples.english: As long as you see your mother, your baby will not cry.

title: Expressing 'only after' with 'cai'
explanation: Expressing 'only after' with 'cai'. Used for indicating conditions.

examples.chinese: 我花了八千块才买到了旅游的东西。
examples.pinyin: wǒ huā le bā qiān kuài cái mǎi dào le lv̌yóu de dōngxī 。
examples.english: It took me 8,000 yuan to buy things for travel.

examples.chinese: 小明花了很多心思才设计出了这张海报。
examples.pinyin: xiǎomíng huā le hěn duō xīnsī cái shèjì chū le zhè zhāng hǎibào 。
examples.english: It took Xiao Ming a lot of thought to design this poster.

examples.chinese: 他用了很多年才原谅了他的妈妈。
examples.pinyin: tā yòng le hěn duō nián cái yuánliàng le tāde māmā 。
examples.english: It took him many years to forgive his mother.

examples.chinese: 那本书我看了两遍才看懂。
examples.pinyin: nà běn shū wǒ kàn le liǎng biàn cái kàn dǒng 。
examples.english: I read that book twice before I understood it.

examples.chinese: 我去了三次北京才见到了那个客户。
examples.pinyin: wǒ qù le sān cì běijīng cái jiàn dào le nàgè kèhù 。
examples.english: I went to Beijing three times before I saw the customer.

title: Expressing 'only if' with 'zhiyou'
structure: 只有 + Essential Condition + 才 + Desired Outcome
explanation: Expressing 'only if' with 'zhiyou'. Used for indicating conditions, indicating cause and effect.

examples.chinese: 只有努力才能做好工作。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyǒu nǔlì cái néng zuò hǎo gōngzuò 。
examples.english: Only work hard to do a good job.

examples.pinyin: zhǐyǒu duō chīfàn cái néng chánggāo 。
examples.english: Only by eating more can you grow taller.

examples.chinese: 只有多说，你的中文才会提高。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyǒu duō shuō，nǐdezhōngwén cái hùi tígāo 。
examples.english: Only by speaking more will your Chinese improve.

examples.chinese: 只有大家一起做才能成功。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyǒu dàjiā yīqǐ zuò cái néng chénggōng 。
examples.english: Only if you do it together can you succeed.

examples.chinese: 只有写完你的作业才能看电视。
examples.pinyin: zhǐyǒu xiě wán nǐde zuòyè cái néng kàn diànshì 。
examples.english: Only after finishing your homework can you watch TV.

title: Using 'youde' to mean 'some'
structure: 有的 + (Subj.) + Predicate， 有的 + (Subj.) + Predicate
explanation: Using 'youde' to mean 'some'. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.chinese: 外国人有的很有钱，有的没钱。
examples.pinyin: wàiguórén yǒude hěn yǒuqián， yǒude méiqián 。
examples.english: Some foreigners are rich and some have no money.

examples.chinese: 我们公司有一些电脑，有的是新的，有的是旧的。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen gōngsī yǒu yīxiē diànnǎo， yǒude shì xīnde，yǒude shì jìude 。
examples.english: Our company has some computers, some are new and some are old.

examples.chinese: 他写了很多书，有的卖得很好，有的卖得不好。
examples.pinyin: tā xiě le hěnduō shū，yǒude mài dé hěnhǎo， yǒude mài dé bùhǎo 。
examples.english: He wrote a lot of books, some sold well and some did not.

examples.chinese: 中国菜有的好吃，有的不好吃。
examples.pinyin: zhōngguócài yǒude hǎochī，yǒude bù hǎochī 。
examples.english: Some Chinese food is delicious and some are not.

examples.chinese: 这家店的衣服有的贵，有的便宜。
examples.pinyin: zhè jiā diàn de yīfú yǒude gùi ， yǒude biànyí 。
examples.english: Some clothes in this shop are expensive and some are cheap.

title: Expressing 'not even one'
structure: 一 + Measure Word + (Noun) + 也 / 都 + 不 / 没 + Verb
explanation: Expressing 'not even one'. Used for describing things.

examples.pinyin: tā yī jù huà dū méi shuō 。
examples.english: He said nothing.

examples.pinyin: tā yī jù zhōngwén dū bù hùi shuō 。
examples.english: He can't speak a sentence of Chinese.

examples.chinese: 为什么这里一个人都没有？
examples.pinyin: wèishíyāo zhèlǐ yī gè rén dū méiyǒu ？
examples.english: Why is there no one here?

examples.pinyin: wǒ yī kǒu dū méi chī 。
examples.english: I didn't eat a bite.

examples.chinese: 来上海以前，他一个外国朋友都没有。
examples.pinyin: lái shànghǎi yǐqián ， tā yī gè wàiguó péngyǒu dū méiyǒu 。
examples.english: Before coming to Shanghai, he had no foreign friends.

title: Expressing 'the more... the more...' with 'yue… yue…'
explanation: Expressing 'the more... the more...' with 'yue… yue…'. Used for expressing degree.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen yuè xiǎng yuè shēngqì 。
examples.english: The more we think, the more angry we are.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yuè kàn yuè xǐhuān 。
examples.english: The more I look, the more I like it.

examples.pinyin: tā yuè pǎo yuè kuài 。
examples.english: He runs faster and faster.

examples.pinyin: yǔ yuè xià yuè dà 。
examples.english: The rain is getting heavier.

examples.pinyin: tā yuè shuō ，wǒ yuè shēngqì 。
examples.english: The more she said, the more angry I was.

title: Using 'ba' sentences
structure: Subj. + [Verb Phrase] + Obj.  /  Subj. + 把 + Obj. + [Verb Phrase]
explanation: Using 'ba' sentences. Used for expressing passive voice.

examples.pinyin: bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzǐ shàng 。
examples.english: Put the book on the table.

examples.chinese: 她把我的手机放在她的包里了。
examples.pinyin: tā bǎ wǒ de shǒujī fàng zài tā de bāo lǐ le 。
examples.english: She put my mobile phone in her bag.

examples.chinese: 他把脏衣服放在床下面了。
examples.pinyin: tā bǎ zāng yīfú fàng zài chuáng xiàmiàn le 。
examples.english: He put the dirty clothes under the bed.

examples.chinese: 你是不是把护照放在行李箱里了？
examples.pinyin: nǐ shì bù shì bǎ hùzhào fàng zài xínglǐxiāng lǐ le ？
examples.english: Did you put your passport in the suitcase?

examples.pinyin: wǒ bǎ zuòyè zuò wán le 。
examples.english: I finished my homework.

title: Using 'bei' sentences
structure: Subj. + 被 (+ Doer) + Verb + 了
explanation: Using 'bei' sentences. Used for sentence patterns, expressing passive voice.

examples.pinyin: règǒu bèi nánhái chī le 。
examples.english: The hot dog was eaten by the boy.

examples.pinyin: règǒu bèi chī le 。
examples.english: The hot dog was eaten.

examples.pinyin: tā bèi dǎ le 。
examples.english: He was beaten.

examples.pinyin: nǐ bèi gōngsī chǎoyóuyú le ？
examples.english: Are you fired by the company?

examples.pinyin: wǒ de shǒujī bèi tōu le 。
examples.english: My phone was stolen.

title: Alternative existential sentences
explanation: Alternative existential sentences. Used for expressing existence.

examples.pinyin: zhuōzǐ shàng fàng zháo yī běn shū 。
examples.english: There is a book on the table.

examples.pinyin: dàtīng lǐ zhàn zháo yīxiē jǐngchá 。
examples.english: There are some policemen standing in the hall.

examples.chinese: 教室里坐着两百多个学生。
examples.pinyin: jiàoshì lǐ zuò zháo liǎng bǎi duō gè xuéshēng 。
examples.english: More than two hundred students sat in the classroom.

examples.chinese: 地下室里堆着很多旧东西。
examples.pinyin: dìxiàshì lǐ dūi zháo hěn duō jìu dōngxī 。
examples.english: There are many old things piled in the basement.

examples.pinyin: lóuxià tíng zháo jī liàng chē 。
examples.english: There are a few cars parked downstairs.

title: Expressing 'as one likes' with 'jiu'
explanation: Expressing 'as one likes' with 'jiu'. Used for expressing attitude, expressing desires.

examples.pinyin: xiǎng chī jìu chī 。
examples.english: Eat if you want.

examples.pinyin: xiǎng zǒu jìu zǒu ba 。
examples.english: Just go if you want to go.

examples.pinyin: xiǎng mǎi jìu mǎi ，bùyòng wèn wǒ 。
examples.english: Buy it if you want, without asking me.

examples.pinyin: bié hàipà ，xiǎng shuō jìu shuō 。
examples.english: Don't be afraid, just talk.

examples.chinese: 想做就做，考虑那么多干吗？
examples.pinyin: xiǎng zuò jìu zuò ，kǎolv̀ nàyāo duō gānma ？
examples.english: Just do it if you want, why do you think so much?

title: Expressing 'every' with question words
explanation: Expressing 'every' with question words. Used for expressing quantity.

examples.pinyin: shúi dū xǐhuān měishí 。
examples.english: Everyone likes food.

examples.pinyin: shúi dū kěyǐ jìn 。
examples.english: Anyone can enter.

examples.pinyin: shúi dū bù xiāngxìn tā shuō de huà 。
examples.english: No one believed what he said.

examples.chinese: 他的房间里哪儿都是脏衣服。
examples.pinyin: tā de fángjiān lǐ nǎér dū shì zāng yīfú 。
examples.english: Dirty clothes are everywhere in his room.

examples.chinese: 我太累了，哪儿都不想去。
examples.pinyin: wǒ tài lèi le，nǎér dū bù xiǎng qù 。
examples.english: I'm too tired to go anywhere.

title: Expressing 'more and more' with 'yuelaiyue'
structure: Subj. + 越来越 + Verb / Adj. + 了
explanation: Expressing 'more and more' with 'yuelaiyue'. Used for expressing degree.

examples.pinyin: tiānqì yuèláiyuè lěng le 。
examples.english: The weather is getting cold.

examples.pinyin: nǐ nv̌ér yuèláiyuè piāoliàng le 。
examples.english: Your daughter is getting more and more beautiful.

examples.pinyin: nǐ shuō dé yuèláiyuè hǎo le 。
examples.english: You are getting better and better.

examples.pinyin: jìzhú yuèláiyuè fādá le 。
examples.english: Technology is becoming more and more developed.

examples.chinese: 空气污染越来越严重了。
examples.pinyin: kōngqì wūrǎn yuèláiyuè yánzhòng le 。
examples.english: Air pollution is getting worse.

title: Expressing purpose with 'weile'
explanation: Expressing purpose with 'weile'. Used for expressing purpose.

examples.chinese: 为了钱，他什么都愿意做。
examples.pinyin: wèile qián ，tā shíyāo dū yuànyì zuò 。
examples.english: For money, he is willing to do anything.

examples.chinese: 为了孩子的未来，他决定搬到上海。
examples.pinyin: wèile háizǐ de wèilái ，tā juédìng bāndào shànghǎi 。
examples.english: For the child's future, he decided to move to Shanghai.

examples.chinese: 为了这个面试，他买了一套很贵的西服。
examples.pinyin: wèile zhègè miànshì ，tā mǎi le yī tào hěn gùi de xīfú 。
examples.english: For this interview, he bought an expensive suit.

examples.chinese: 为了找灵感，那个作家搬到了农村。
examples.pinyin: wèile zhǎo línggǎn ，nàgè zuòjiā bāndào le nóngcūn 。
examples.english: In order to find inspiration, the writer moved to the countryside.

examples.chinese: 五年前，为了女朋友，他放弃了上海的工作。
examples.pinyin: wǔnián qián ，wèile nv̌péngyǒu ，tā fàngqì le shànghǎi de gōngzuò 。
examples.english: Five years ago, he gave up his job in Shanghai for his girlfriend.

title: Expressing 'some' with question words
explanation: Expressing 'some' with question words

examples.pinyin: zhègè rén wǒ zài nǎér jiàn guò 。
examples.english: Where have I seen this person?

examples.pinyin: zhè shǒu gē wǒ zài nǎér tīng guò 。
examples.english: Where have I heard this song?

examples.chinese: 这篇文章我们在哪儿看过。
examples.pinyin: zhè piān wénzhāng wǒmen zài nǎér kàn guò 。
examples.english: Where have we read this article?

examples.pinyin: nǐ ràng shúi lái bāng wǒ yīxià 。
examples.english: Who do you want to help me with?

examples.pinyin: zhè jiàn shì wǒ tīng shúi shuō guò 。
examples.english: Who have I heard about this?

title: Expressing comparable degree with 'you'
structure: A + 有 + B + Adj. + 吗 ？
explanation: Expressing comparable degree with 'you'. Used for comparing, describing people, describing things.

examples.pinyin: tā yǒu wǒ shuài ma ？
examples.english: Is he handsome?

examples.pinyin: nǐ yǒu lǎobǎn máng ma ？
examples.english: Are you busy with your boss?

examples.pinyin: tā yǒu yáomíng gāo ma ？
examples.english: Does he have Yao Minggao?

examples.chinese: 纽约人口有上海人口多吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐuyuē rénkǒu yǒu shànghǎi rénkǒu duō ma ？
examples.english: Does New York have a larger population than Shanghai?

examples.pinyin: tā yǒu wǒ zuò dé hǎo ma ？
examples.english: Did he do well with me?

title: Expressing 'compared with' using 'gen'
structure: 跟 + A + 比 (起来) ，B⋯⋯  /  B + 跟 + A + 比 (起来) ，⋯⋯
explanation: Expressing 'compared with' using 'gen'. Used for comparing.

examples.chinese: 跟上海比，我老家的物价很低。
examples.pinyin: gēn shànghǎi bǐ ，wǒ lǎojiā de wùjià hěn dī 。
examples.english: Compared with Shanghai, the price in my hometown is very low.

examples.chinese: 跟东部比，我更喜欢美国西部。
examples.pinyin: gēn dōngbù bǐ ，wǒ gèng xǐhuān měiguó xībù 。
examples.english: I like the American West more than the East.

examples.chinese: 跟他比，你最大的优势是什么？
examples.pinyin: gēn tā bǐ ，nǐ zùidà de yōushì shì shíyāo ？
examples.english: Compared with him, what is your biggest advantage?

examples.chinese: 妹妹跟姐姐比起来有点害羞。
examples.pinyin: mèimèi gēn jiějiě bǐqǐlái yǒudiǎn hàixīu 。
examples.english: The sister is a little shy compared to her sister.

examples.chinese: 新版本跟旧版本比起来，多了一些功能。
examples.pinyin: xīn bǎnběn gēn jìu bǎnběn bǐqǐlái ，duō le yīxiē gōngnéng 。
examples.english: Compared with the old version, the new version has some more functions.

title: Expressing 'even more' with 'geng' or 'hai'
structure: Noun 1 + 比 + Noun 2 + 更 / 还 + Adj.
explanation: Expressing 'even more' with 'geng' or 'hai'. Used for comparing, adding more explanation, expressing degree.

examples.pinyin: shànghǎi bǐ nǐuyuē rénkǒu gèng duō 。
examples.english: Shanghai has a larger population than New York.

examples.pinyin: wǒ mèimèi bǐ wǒ gèng shòu 。
examples.english: My sister is thinner than me.

examples.chinese: 我的猫比我的狗更淘气。
examples.pinyin: wǒ de māo bǐ wǒ de gǒu gèng táoqì 。
examples.english: My cat is more naughty than my dog.

examples.chinese: 有时候动物比人类更聪明。
examples.pinyin: yǒushíhòu dòngwù bǐ rénlèi gèng cōngmíng 。
examples.english: Sometimes animals are smarter than humans.

examples.chinese: 大城市的工作机会比我老家更多。
examples.pinyin: dà chéngshì de gōngzuò jīhùi bǐ wǒ lǎojiā gèng duō 。
examples.english: There are more job opportunities in big cities than in my hometown.

title: Expressing 'much more' in comparisons
structure: Noun 1 + 比 + Noun 2 + Adj. + 很多 / 得多 / 多了
explanation: Expressing 'much more' in comparisons. Used for comparing, adding more explanation, expressing quality.

examples.pinyin: pīnyīn bǐ hànzì róngyì hěnduō 。
examples.english: Pinyin is much easier than Chinese characters.

examples.chinese: 坐高铁比坐飞机方便很多。
examples.pinyin: zuò gāotiě bǐ zuò fēijī fāngbiàn hěnduō 。
examples.english: It is much more convenient to take the high-speed train than to take the plane.

examples.chinese: 这个女老师比那个男老师严格得多。
examples.pinyin: zhè gè nv̌lǎoshī bǐ nàgè nánlǎoshī yángé dé duō 。
examples.english: This female teacher is much stricter than that male teacher.

examples.chinese: 我老婆的工资比我高得多。
examples.pinyin: wǒ lǎopó de gōngzī bǐ wǒ gāo déduō 。
examples.english: My wife's salary is much higher than mine.

examples.pinyin: nǐ bǐ wǒ yǒujīngyàn duōle 。
examples.english: You have more experience than me.

title: Expressing 'except' and 'in addition' with 'chule… yiwai'
structure: 除了 ⋯⋯ (+ 以外) ，Subj. + 都 / 也 / 还 ⋯⋯
explanation: Expressing 'except' and 'in addition' with 'chule… yiwai'. Used for giving alternatives.

examples.pinyin: chúle tā ，wǒmen dū qù guò 。
examples.english: We have all been except him.

examples.chinese: 除了白酒，别的酒他都喝。
examples.pinyin: chúle báijǐu ，biéde jǐu tā dū hē 。
examples.english: He drinks everything except liquor.

examples.chinese: 除了周末，老板每天都加班。
examples.pinyin: chúle zhōumò ，lǎobǎn měitiān dū jiābān 。
examples.english: The boss works overtime every day except on weekends.

examples.chinese: 除了价格以外，其他方面我们都很满意。
examples.pinyin: chúle jiàgé yǐwài ，qítā fāngmiàn wǒmen dū hěn mǎnyì 。
examples.english: In addition to the price, we are very satisfied with other aspects.

examples.chinese: 除了政治新闻以外，其他新闻我都看。
examples.pinyin: chúle zhèngzhì xīnwén yǐwài ，qítā xīnwén wǒ dū kàn 。
examples.english: I read all news except political news.

title: Expressing 'if... then...' with 'ruguo... jiu...'
structure: 如果 ⋯⋯ ，就 / 那么 / 那 ⋯⋯
explanation: Expressing 'if... then...' with 'ruguo... jiu...'. Used for indicating conditions, expressing possibility.

examples.chinese: 如果有困难，就给我打电话。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ yǒu kùnnán ，jìu gěi wǒ dǎ diànhuà 。
examples.english: If you have difficulty, call me.

examples.chinese: 如果你输了，就给我一百块。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ nǐ shū le ，jìu gěi wǒ yī bǎi kuài 。
examples.english: If you lose, give me a hundred dollars.

examples.chinese: 如果真的找不回来，就再买一个吧。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ zhēnde zhǎo bù húilái ，jìu zài mǎi yī gè ba 。
examples.english: If you ca n’t find it, buy another one.

examples.chinese: 如果他再来，我们就报警。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ tā zài lái ，wǒmen jìu bàojǐng 。
examples.english: If he comes again, we will call the police.

examples.chinese: 如果你们已经不相爱了，就分手吧。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ nǐmen yǐjīng bù xiāngài le ，jìu fēnshǒu ba 。
examples.english: If you are not in love anymore, just break up.

title: Expressing 'if' with 'ruguo... dehua'
explanation: Expressing 'if' with 'ruguo... dehua'. Used for indicating conditions, expressing possibility.

examples.chinese: 如果明天下雨的话，我们就不去了。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ míngtiān xiàyǔ dehuà ，wǒmen jìu bù qù le 。
examples.english: If it rains tomorrow, we will not go.

examples.pinyin: bié diǎn nàyāo duō cài 。
examples.english: Don't order so many dishes.

examples.chinese: 如果有人帮他的话，他就不会出事了。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ yǒu rén bāng tā dehuà ，tā jìu bù hùi chūshì le 。
examples.english: If someone helps him, he wo n’t be in trouble.

examples.chinese: 如果你们不来的话，一定会后悔的。
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ nǐmen bù lái dehuà ，yīdìng hùi hòuhǔi de 。
examples.english: If you do not come, you will regret it.

examples.chinese: 如果他不同意的话，你怎么办？
examples.pinyin: rúguǒ tā bù tóngyì dehuà ，nǐ zěnyāobàn ？
examples.english: What if you do n’t agree?

title: Expressing 'not only... but also'
explanation: Expressing 'not only... but also'. Used for adding more explanation, connecting ideas.

examples.chinese: 这个东西不但贵，而且难买。
examples.pinyin: zhègè dōngxī bùdàn gùi ，érqiě nán mǎi 。
examples.english: This thing is not only expensive but also difficult to buy.

examples.chinese: 这道菜不但好看，也好吃。
examples.pinyin: zhè dào cài bùdàn hǎokàn ，yě hǎochī 。
examples.english: This dish is not only beautiful, but also delicious.

examples.chinese: 她不但聪明，而且很幽默。
examples.pinyin: tā bùdàn cōngmíng ，érqiě hěn yōumò 。
examples.english: She is not only smart, but also very humorous.

examples.chinese: 她不但离开了我，还拿走了我所有的钱。
examples.pinyin: tā bùdàn líkāi le wǒ ，huán názǒu le wǒ suǒyǒu de qián 。
examples.english: Not only did she leave me, she also took all my money.

examples.chinese: 他不但提前做完了，而且做得很好。
examples.pinyin: tā bùdàn tíqián zuò wán le ，érqiě zuò dé hěn hǎo 。
examples.english: Not only did he finish ahead of time, he did well.

title: Comparing 'cai' and 'jiu'
explanation: Comparing 'cai' and 'jiu'. Used for sequencing events in time, expressing time and date, expressing attitude.

examples.chinese: 我等了两个小时才买到票。
examples.pinyin: wǒ děng le liǎng gè xiǎoshí cái mǎidào piào 。
examples.english: I waited two hours before buying the ticket.

examples.chinese: 老板十一点才到办公室。
examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn shíyī diǎn cái dào bàngōngshì 。
examples.english: The boss arrived at the office at eleven o'clock.

examples.chinese: 这个项目最少要花两个月才能做完。
examples.pinyin: zhègè xiàngmù zùishǎo yào huā liǎng gè yuè cái néng zuò wán 。
examples.english: This project will take at least two months to complete.

examples.chinese: 我们有这么多人，你才点了三个菜。
examples.pinyin: wǒmen yǒu zhèyāo duō rén，nǐ cái diǎn le sān gè cài 。
examples.english: We have so many people, you just ordered three dishes.

examples.chinese: 我买了这么多东西，才花了五百块。
examples.pinyin: wǒ mǎi le zhèyāo duō dōngxī，cái huā le wǔ bǎi kuài 。
examples.english: I bought so many things, it only cost 500 yuan.

title: Comparing 'gang' and 'gangcai'
explanation: Comparing 'gang' and 'gangcai'. Used for describing actions, expressing time and date.

examples.pinyin: tā gāng dào 。
examples.english: He just arrived.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen zuótiān gāng dào 。
examples.english: We just arrived yesterday.

examples.pinyin: zhēn bùqiǎo ，lǎobǎn gāng zǒu 。
examples.english: What a coincidence, the boss just left.

examples.chinese: 我刚到上海的时候，谁都不认识。
examples.pinyin: wǒ gāng dào shànghǎi de shíhòu ，shúi dū bù rènshì 。
examples.english: When I first arrived in Shanghai, no one knew.

examples.pinyin: tā de gǎnmào gānggāng hǎo 。
examples.english: His cold is just right.

title: Comparing 'youdian' and 'yidian'
explanation: Comparing 'youdian' and 'yidian'. Used for adding more explanation, expressing attitude, expressing degree.

examples.pinyin: jīntiān wǒ yǒudiǎn lèi 。
examples.english: Today I am a little tired.

examples.pinyin: zhègè cài yǒudiǎn xián 。
examples.english: This dish is a bit salty.

examples.pinyin: qǐng shuō màn yīdiǎn 。
examples.english: Please speak slower.

examples.pinyin: kuài diǎn，yào chídào le 。
examples.english: Hurry up and be late.

examples.pinyin: lǎobǎn ，biànyí diǎn ba 。
examples.english: Boss, cheaper.

title: Comparing 'zai' and 'you'
explanation: Comparing 'zai' and 'you'. Used for describing actions, sequencing events in time.

examples.pinyin: wǒmen xiàcì zài lái ba 。
examples.english: Let's come again next time.

examples.pinyin: wǒ xiǎng zài kàn yīxià 。
examples.english: I want to take another look.

examples.pinyin: qǐng zài shuō yī cì 。
examples.english: Please say that again.

examples.pinyin: wǒ yào zài kàn yī bù diànyǐng 。
examples.english: I want to watch another movie.

examples.chinese: 你可以再做一碗面条吗？
examples.pinyin: nǐ kěyǐ zài zuò yī wǎn miàntiáo ma ？
examples.english: Can you make another bowl of noodles?
